name: Container Debug with Ephemeral Containers
description: Debug running containers by attaching an ephemeral debug shell or inspecting container internals without modifying the original image.
trendingReason: Distroless and minimal images make debugging harder without these techniques

steps.title: List running containers
steps.description: Identify the container you want to debug.

steps.title: Exec into a running container
steps.description: Open an interactive shell inside a running container.
steps.tip: Use /bin/sh if /bin/bash is not available (common in Alpine-based images).

steps.title: Run a debug sidecar with full tools
steps.description: Attach a debug container that shares the network and PID namespace of the target container.
steps.tip: nicolaka/netshoot includes curl, dig, nslookup, tcpdump, iptables, and many more network tools.

steps.title: Copy files from a container for inspection
steps.description: Extract files from a container to your local machine for analysis.

steps.title: View container resource usage in real time
steps.description: Monitor CPU, memory, and network I/O of running containers.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Container Debug Workflow
set -euo pipefail

CONTAINER=${1:?"Usage: $0 <container-name>"}

echo "=== Container Info ==="
docker inspect "$CONTAINER" --format '{{.State.Status}} | Image: {{.Config.Image}} | PID: {{.State.Pid}}'

echo ""
echo "=== Container Logs (last 20 lines) ==="
docker logs --tail 20 "$CONTAINER"

echo ""
echo "=== Container Processes ==="
docker top "$CONTAINER"

echo ""
echo "=== Container Resource Usage ==="
docker stats --no-stream "$CONTAINER"

echo ""
echo "=== Debug Commands ==="
echo "Shell:     docker exec -it $CONTAINER /bin/sh"
echo "Netshoot:  docker run -it --rm --pid=container:$CONTAINER --net=container:$CONTAINER nicolaka/netshoot"
echo "Copy logs: docker cp $CONTAINER:/var/log/ ./container-logs/"

faq.question: What if the container has no shell?
faq.answer: Use the netshoot sidecar technique or 'docker export' to create a tarball of the filesystem you can inspect locally.

faq.question: How do I debug a container that keeps crashing?
faq.answer: Use 'docker logs <container>' to see the crash output. Override the entrypoint with 'docker run -it --entrypoint /bin/sh <image>' to get a shell before the app starts.

faq.question: Can I attach a debugger to a container process?
faq.answer: Yes. Use 'docker exec' with the debugger CLI, or map a debug port when starting the container with -p flag. For Node.js: --inspect=0.0.0.0:9229.
